Metric: Software name? (string)
URL? (URL)
Affiliation (institution(s)) (string or N/A)
Response: PickNik Robotics (PickNik Inc.) leads development; originally developed by Sachin Chitta and Dave Coleman at Willow Garage / SRI International; maintained by broad ROS community

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Open-source robotics manipulation framework for ROS and ROS 2; provides motion planning, kinematics, collision checking, trajectory execution, and perception integration for robot arms and manipulators; integrates OMPL, CHOMP, STOMP, and other planners as plugins

Metric: Number of developers (all developers that have contributed at least one commit to the project) (use repo commit logs) (number)
Response: ~200 (moveit2 repository; combined moveit and moveit2 repos have even more contributors; broad ROS community participation)

Metric: How is the project funded? (unfunded, unclear, funded∗) where ∗ requires a string to say the source of funding
Response: funded (PickNik Robotics company revenue; Google Summer of Code participation; industrial sponsor contributions from companies using MoveIt; MoveIt Pro commercial product funds open-source development)

Metric: Initial release date? (date)
Response: 2011 (MoveIt 1.0 for ROS; created by Sachin Chitta at Willow Garage; MoveIt 2 for ROS 2 began circa 2018)

Metric: Last commit date? (date)
Response: 2025-02 (actively developed; MoveIt 2 version 2.12.x maintained; regular releases)

column4: MoveIt! is the de facto standard robotics manipulation framework in ROS. Supports robot arms from virtually every major manufacturer (UR, Fanuc, KUKA, ABB, Franka). URDF/SRDF based robot description. Integrates with Gazebo, RViz, and real hardware via ros_control. Annual World MoveIt Day community event. MoveIt Pro (commercial) builds on open-source MoveIt 2.

column4: Installation is simple once ROS 2 is set up (single apt command). ROS 2 prerequisite installation itself involves several steps (adding apt repo, keys, etc.) but is well-documented. MoveIt 2 documentation is excellent with step-by-step Docker option for a simpler alternative.
